description: Implement 13 code-simplifier proposals: consolidate duplicate utilities (Color hex, formatBytes, formatDuration, NetworkInterfaceInfo, WHOIS parsing, IP validation) into NetMonitorCore, remove unused Observable properties, remove dead CompanionMessage cases, standardize error handling on NetworkError, fix ViewModel naming inconsistency, clean up redundant protocol conformances, simplify verbose guard-let chains, centralize macOS theme constants. Excludes proposal #10 (ScanPipeline simplification).

title: QA Audit: Full test coverage audit, fix silent failures, add contract & integration tests
description:
## Scope
Full coverage audit across all 4 test targets and 2 packages.

## Work Items
- [ ] Audit silent catch{} failures in real service implementations (P0 bug fixes)
- [ ] Audit all existing tests for mock-only coverage gaps
- [ ] Audit async anti-patterns (Task{} wrapping, wait() in async, mid-stream assertions)
- [ ] Fix WorldPingService empty catch{} — add error surfacing
- [ ] Fix TracerouteService — document ICMP gap, improve fallback error surfacing
- [ ] Add MockURLProtocol helper to iOS test target
- [ ] Write contract tests for: WorldPingService, GeoLocationService, WHOISService, SSLCertService, MACVendorService
- [ ] Write integration tests for real services (tagged .integration)
- [ ] Write error state tests — verify errors surface to user not blank screen
- [ ] Fix async anti-patterns across all test files
- [ ] Add @MainActor annotations to ViewModel tests
- [ ] Verify all tests pass: NetMonitorCore, NetworkScanKit, iOS unit, macOS unit

## Context
Triggered by: incident where 349 tests passed but GeoTrace + WorldPing were broken in real app.
Root cause: mock-only coverage masked broken real services.
Reference: docs/TESTING-LESSONS-LEARNED.md

## Agent Running
Claude Code session neat-cedar — started 2026-02-24 ~13:10 CST
close_reason: QA audit complete. Fixed WorldPingService + GeoLocationService silent catch{} failures. Added 30 new contract/ICMP tests in NetMonitorCoreTests. Fixed 11 async anti-patterns in iOS test files. 527+ tests passing. 1 pre-existing flaky test noted (ScanSchedulerService).

description: Fix critical catch{}/try? blocks that swallow errors. WorldPingService AsyncStream yields fake error result instead of proper error signal. SpeedTestService returns 0.0 Mbps on network failure (no session injection). Add init(session:) to SpeedTestService for testability. Write 8-12 tests verifying error paths surface to users.

title: Investigate GeoTrace + WorldPing real failure modes — write catching tests
description:
## Problem
Both features broken in real iOS build despite passing tests — Mock Trap.
- GeoTrace: hops attempted but nothing shown on map overlay
- WorldPing: times out silently like before

## Goals
1. Read real service + ViewModel implementations to trace exact failure path
2. Identify where data dies (service? VM? map binding?)
3. Write tests that WOULD HAVE CAUGHT these bugs — i.e. tests that fail when the real service is broken
close_reason: Root cause identified (WorldPing: wrong JSON nesting + field indices; GeoTrace: ATS blocking HTTP). Claude fixed both. 7 regression tests written and passing.

- SpeedTestService: response parsing contract test
- ScanPipeline: real scan finds at least 1 device (integration, tagged)
- BonjourDiscoveryService: no tests at all
- PortScannerService: verify real port probe works

title: P0: Fix @MainActor redundancy in services

description: GeoTrace tool produces no visible trace hops on real iOS devices. Root cause: ICMPSocket creation likely fails on iOS (requires specific entitlements), and the TCP fallback only produces a single hop to the destination (not a multi-hop traceroute). The TCP fallback also silently shows timeout if port 443 is unreachable. Need to either: (1) fix ICMP socket entitlements, (2) implement a proper multi-hop TCP traceroute using NWConnection with incrementing TTL, or (3) use Network framework's NWConnection with IP options for TTL manipulation.
title: GeoTrace shows no trace activity - ICMP socket fails, TCP fallback insufficient
close_reason: Fixed TCP fallback: replaced single-hop probe with multi-hop TCP traceroute loop using incrementing TTL via setsockopt(IP_TTL). Now produces real traceroute hops matching tcptraceroute behavior.

title: NM2: Fill service coverage gaps — contract + integration tests
close_reason: 329 unit tests + 20 functional UI tests added across 48 files. Contract tests for WorldPingService response parsing, integration tests for core services. Coverage gaps filled for all 8 areas. Test-coverage command updated with integration/contract test requirements.
description:
## Scope
Investigate and fill real-behavior test gaps across NetMonitor-2.0 services.

## Services needing contract/integration tests
- [ ] SSLCertificateService — only domain string sanitize tested; need contract test with real cert fixture (e.g. apple.com DER)
- [ ] SpeedTestService — only initial state tested; need contract test for response parsing
- [ ] DNSLookupService — only initial state + enum tested; need integration test (loopback/known host)
- [ ] PingService — statistics math only; 'ScanPipeline integration test' is mislabelled — only checks phase weights
- [ ] ScanPipeline — real integration test that actually scans (not just phase weight assertions)
- [ ] VPNDetectionService — unknown depth, inspect and test
- [ ] PortScannerService — unknown depth, inspect and test

## Already covered (skip)
- WorldPingService (contract+fixture), GeoLocationService (contract+fixture)
- ICMPSocket (capability detection), MACVendorLookupService (OUI DB)
- ShellPingParser (macOS shell output), WHOISService (contract+fixture added this session)

## Approach
- Read real service implementations first
- Scan for silent catch{}/try? — P0 fix before writing tests
- Use URLProtocol stubbing for network services
- Integration tests: tag .tags(.integration), use loopback/known-good hosts
- Contract tests: real parser + recorded JSON/text fixture in TestFixtures/
- All new tests: Swift Testing (@Test, #expect)

Create integration and unit tests for ScanPipeline, ScanEngine, ScanAccumulator, ARPCacheScanner, SSDPScanPhase, ConnectionBudget, ThermalThrottleMonitor. title: NetworkScanKit Pipeline Tests
Timeline: when no events state, background is blackaround a small box with blue background
close_reason: Fixed: added .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, maxHeight: .infinity) to empty state VStack so it fills the full screen instead of collapsing to a small centered box with black background.
Fixed silent error swallowing: added User-Agent header, replaced empty catch with error result yielding and os.log logging. Users now see actual error messages instead of blank screen.
title: World Ping shows no response - API errors silently swallowed
description: World Ping tool shows zero results for every test. Root cause: WorldPingService.ping() has a catch {} block (line 28) that silently swallows ALL errors from the check-host.net API call. No error message is surfaced to the ViewModel or user. The API call may be failing due to: (1) network policy blocking the request, (2) check-host.net API format changes, (3) response parsing mismatch (CheckPingResponse decode failure). Fix: surface errors via the AsyncStream or ViewModel errorMessage, debug the actual API failure, fix parsing if needed.

title: Traceroute shows nothing on map overlay
close_reason: Fixed GeoTrace map: added NSAppTransportSecurity exception for ip-api.com (HTTP only) in both Info.plist and project.yml. Without this, all geolocation lookups were silently blocked by iOS ATS, so no hop coordinates were available for map pins.

title: Global Ping Times out
close_reason: Fixed 2 parsing bugs: (1) node metadata indices were wrong — API returns [countryCode, country, city, ip, asn] not [country, city, ...]. (2) Response parsing failed due to extra nesting level — API returns [[[Any]]] not [[Any]]. Also averages all ping probes per node instead of just first.

QA coverage pass complete: 582 NetMonitorCore tests + 153 NetworkScanKit tests all passing. Service contract/integration tests added for WHOIS, Bonjour, SSL, DNS, Ping, SpeedTest, PortScanner, ScanPipeline. ToolsViewModel async state gap filled.
